name: AN INTERVENTIONAL EFFICACY AND SAFETY, PHASE 3, DOUBLE-BLIND, 2-ARM STUDY TO INVESTIGATE ORALLY ADMINISTERED IBUZATRELVIR COMPARED WITH PLACEBO IN NON-HOSPITALIZED SYMPTOMATIC ADULT AND ADOLESCENT PARTICIPANTS WITH COVID-19 WHO ARE AT HIGH RISK OF PROGRESSING TO SEVERE ILLNESS

titles.text: AN INTERVENTIONAL EFFICACY AND SAFETY, PHASE 3, DOUBLE-BLIND, 2-ARM STUDY TO INVESTIGATE ORALLY ADMINISTERED IBUZATRELVIR COMPARED WITH PLACEBO IN NON-HOSPITALIZED SYMPTOMATIC ADULT AND ADOLESCENT PARTICIPANTS WITH COVID-19 WHO ARE AT HIGH RISK OF PROGRESSING TO SEVERE ILLNESS
A Study to Learn About a Study Medicine Called Ibuzatrelvir in Adult and Adolescent Patients With Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) Who Are Not Hospitalized But Are at Risk For Severe Disease

amendments.name: Amendment 2

changes.name: 2 — Exclusion Criteria
changes.summary: Added new Exclusion Criterion 8 to clarify that patients with a prior history of hypersensitivity to the study intervention or its components should not be enrolled.
changes.rationale: To clarify that participants should be screened for this criterion prior to enrollment. This protocol change supersedes the PACL dated 23 May 2025 so that the current protocol instruction will be clear to the investigator.

changes.name: 2 — Inclusion Criteria
changes.summary: The lower limit for BMI as a risk factor is increased to ≥35 kg/m 2 .
changes.rationale: A BMI of ≥ 35 kg/m 2 is categorized as Class 2 or Class 3 obesity. This higher classification recognizes that the health risks associated with obesity worsen as BMI increases.

changes.name: 2 — Synopsis Section 4.1 Overall Design
changes.summary: Introduction of an approximate 20% cap on the number of participants aged 12 to 49 years.
changes.rationale: To ensure a reasonably balanced distribution across age groups and risk factors

changes.name: 2 — Synopsis Section 4.1 Overall Design
changes.summary: Introduction of an approximate 20% cap on the number of participants aged 50 to 64 years who are not moderately immunocompromised and do not have cardiovascular disease or chronic lung disease or mild to moderate renal impairment.
changes.rationale: To ensure a reasonably balanced distribution across age groups and risk factors.

changes.name: 2 — Clinical Overview Section 6.9 Prior and Concomitant Therapy
changes.summary: Information regarding recently completed Phase 1 clinical studies has been added.
changes.rationale: To provide updated information on completed studies.

changedSections.sectionTitle: Clinical Overview Section 6.9 Prior and Concomitant Therapy

changes.name: 2 — Justification for Dose
changes.summary: New paragraphs on the interaction of ibuzatrelvir with strong CYP3A4 inducer carbamazepine and probe P-gp substrate dabigatran, and on the effect of hepatic and renal impairment have been added.
changes.rationale: To provide new information from completed Studies C5091009, C5091010, C5091014 and C5091015.

changes.name: 2 — Prohibited Concomitant Medications That May Results in DDI
changes.summary: The rationale for prohibiting CYP3A inducers and P-gp substrates with narrow therapeutic indices and the rationale for why CYP3A inhibitors are allowed in this study has been removed from Appendix 8.
changes.rationale: Rationale for prohibiting CYP3A4 inducers and P- gp substrates with narrow therapeutic indices and the rationale for allowing CYP3A4 inhibitors is provided in Section 4.3. To avoid duplication, rationale from Appendix 8 has been deleted.

changedSections.sectionTitle: Prohibited Concomitant Medications That May Results in DDI

changes.name: 2 — Prohibited Concomitant Medications That May Result in DDI
changes.summary: Added colchicine to the Prohibited Medication table in Appendix 8.
changes.rationale: Colchicine is a narrow therapeutic index P-gp substrate and is not explicitly mentioned in the prohibited medication list.

changedSections.sectionTitle: Prohibited Concomitant Medications That May Result in DDI

changes.name: 2 — Inclusion Criteria
changes.summary: Minor update to Inclusion Criterion #3.
changes.rationale: To clarify that SARS- CoV-2 infection may be confirmed by RAT from a nasopharyngeal specimen.

changes.name: 2 — Nasal / NP Swab for Viral Screening Assessment
changes.summary: Updated Section 8.7.2 header to Nasal / NP Swab for Viral Screening Assessment.
changes.rationale: To clarify that nasopharyngeal swabs may be used for the viral screening assessment.

changedSections.sectionTitle: Nasal / NP Swab for Viral Screening Assessment

changes.name: 2 — Rescreening
changes.summary: Language added to clarify that repeating RAT does not constitute rescreening.
changes.rationale: To avoid confusion between rescreening and repeating RAT.

changes.name: 2 — Schedule of Activities
changes.summary: Removed two sample timepoints in SoA Table 1 for Retained Research Sample for Genetics (Prep D1).
changes.rationale: Prep D1 collection is only required once during the study for participants where this sample collection is applicable.

changes.name: 2 — Schedule of Activities
changes.summary: Row added to SoA Table 2 for retained research samples for biomarkers.
changes.rationale: To allow collection of retained research samples at Weeks 12 and 24, and if early termination occurs after Day 34.

changes.name: 2 — Schedule of Activities
changes.summary: Text added to Notes section of SoA tables for Biomarker blood sampling (Table 2) and Participant Diary global impression questions for Long COVID/PASC (Tables 1 and 2) to clarify that this will be performed in adult participants only.
changes.rationale: To be consistent with the collection instructions for the other biomarkers and clarify which questionnaires apply to adults only.

changes.name: 2 — Schedule of Activities
changes.summary: Minor update to Notes section of Table 1 regarding SARS-CoV-2 RNA level assessment (NP).
changes.rationale: 'or at ET' removed since an NP swab is always collected at ET.

changes.name: 2 — Patient Input Into Design
changes.summary: Minor update to text to change 'NP swabs' to 'nasal swabs'.
changes.rationale: To clarify the recommendation obtained during the patient input into study design session.

changes.name: 2 — Clinical Safety Laboratory Assessments
changes.summary: Text added to clarify that laboratory safety parameters will be graded according to the DAIDS Table for Grading the Severity of Adult and Pediatric Adverse Events.
changes.rationale: To add clarity on how to grade laboratory abnormalities.

changes.name: 2 — Clinical Laboratory Tests Table 3
changes.summary: Removal of SARS-CoV-2 anti S IgG + IgM qualitative assessment.
changes.rationale: Qualitative determination of antibodies is not necessary in the presence of a positive quantitative test.

changes.name: 2 — Clinical Laboratory Tests Table 4
changes.summary: Removal of RBC morphology
changes.rationale: RBC morphology is not needed given that ibuzatrelvir does not have the potential to disrupt hematopoiesis.

changes.name: 2 — Synopsis Section 3 Objectives, Endpoints, and Estimands Section 9.3.4 Secondary Endpoint(s)/Estimand(s) Analysis
changes.summary: Update to the secondary endpoint 'Change from baseline in SARS-CoV-2 RNA in NP samples over time' to include 'NP/nasal'.
changes.rationale: To account for the Day 3 nasal swab.

changedSections.sectionTitle: Synopsis Section 3 Objectives, Endpoints, and Estimands Section 9.3.4 Secondary Endpoint(s)/Estimand(s) Analysis

changes.name: 2 — Objectives, Endpoints, and Estimands Section 9.3.5 Tertiary/ Exploratory Endpoint(s) Analysis
changes.summary: Update to tertiary endpoint language
changes.rationale: For clarity

changedSections.sectionTitle: Objectives, Endpoints, and Estimands Section 9.3.5 Tertiary/ Exploratory Endpoint(s) Analysis

changes.name: 2 — Primary Estimand/Coprimary Estimands
changes.summary: Update to text regarding handling of rescue medication intercurrent events for the primary estimand.
changes.rationale: To ensure that when rescue medication is administered not because of worsening COVID-19, data is set to missing instead of simply being censored.

changes.name: 2 — Objectives, Endpoints, and Estimands; Main Analytical Approach
changes.summary: Text added to state that only adjudicated data will be used for the analysis of COVID-19- related emergency department visits and rescue medication administered.
changes.rationale: For clarity

changedSections.sectionTitle: Objectives, Endpoints, and Estimands; Main Analytical Approach

changes.name: 2 — Main Analytical Approach
changes.summary: Scenario added for when missing data will be imputed using multiple imputation.
changes.rationale: To align with updated text for handling of rescue medication intercurrent events.

changes.name: 2 — Primary Estimand/Coprimary Estimands; Key Secondary Estimands; Analysis Sets
changes.summary: ITT analysis set renamed as modified ITT analysis set.
changes.rationale: The original ITT population definition had additional exclusions, and therefore was not a pure ITT population. The analysis set name was changed to modified ITT population, and this will be used for primary and key secondary efficacy analyses. Previous mITT analysis set removed.

changedSections.sectionTitle: Primary Estimand/Coprimary Estimands; Key Secondary Estimands; Analysis Sets

changes.name: 2 — Key Secondary Estimands
changes.summary: Clarifications to second and third key secondary estimands.
changes.rationale: A typographical error was corrected so that CRPE and PACE data for participants who discontinue before Week 24 will be imputed, rather than data for those who discontinue before Day 28. Also added that other appropriate imputation methods may

changes.name: 2 — Analysis Sets; General Considerations; Supplementary Analyses
changes.summary: Added and updated definitions of analysis sets and removed enrolled analysis set. New ITT analysis set will include all participants randomly assigned to study intervention and Full Analysis Set will include participants who take at least 1 dose of study intervention. Removed previous mITT analysis set. Other updates made for clarity.
changes.rationale: Updates to align with statistical analysis plan. The previous mITT analysis set (and associated supplementary analysis) has been removed, as there should be very few additional participants in this analysis set (as those treated more than 5 days after symptom onset should not be enrolled in the study).

changedSections.sectionTitle: Analysis Sets; General Considerations; Supplementary Analyses

changes.name: 2 — Sensitivity Analysis; Supplementary Analyses
changes.summary: New sensitivity analysis added. Sensitivity and supplementary analyses switched in the protocol.
changes.rationale: To allow an assessment of efficacy which assumes all use of rescue medication is considered to be a primary endpoint event (not just rescue use due to worsening COVID-19) - based on regulatory feedback and to align with the SAP. Sensitivity and supplementary analyses switched according to current definitions.

changes.name: 2 — General Considerations Section 9.3.4 Secondary Endpoint(s)/Estimand(s) Analyses
changes.summary: Covariates added for MMRMof secondary endpoints.
changes.rationale: To align with SAP

changedSections.sectionTitle: General Considerations Section 9.3.4 Secondary Endpoint(s)/Estimand(s) Analyses

changes.name: 2 — Tertiary/ Exploratory Endpoint(s) Analyses
changes.summary: Updates to which tertiary endpoints will be included in the CSR.
changes.rationale: Clarified that only descriptive summaries of ibuzatrelvir plasma concentrations will be in the CSR, and other tertiary endpoints will not be included in the CSR.

changes.name: 2 — Interim Analyses; Synopsis; Overall Design
changes.summary: Updates to text regarding the interim analysis.
changes.rationale: To clarify that there will be a single interim analysis for assessing futility for primary endpoint and at the same interim analysis timepoint there will be a blinded psychometric validation of the Long COVID/PASC PRO endpoint.

changes.name: 2 — Interim Analyses
changes.summary: Update to the description of the final type I error rate.
changes.rationale: To clarify that the methods utilized for assessing futility for the Interim Analysis will not change the final overall 2-sided type I error rate.

changes.name: 2 — Interim Analyses
changes.summary: Added blinded, planned review and summaries of Long COVID/PASC symptoms.
changes.rationale: To describe additional summaries of symptoms that are not specifically included in the Long COVID/PASC PRO instrument, in a blinded manner at the interim analysis.

changes.name: 2 — Interim Analyses
changes.summary: Moved statement describing futility rule and SSR as non- binding
changes.rationale: This has been moved later in this section for clarity

changes.name: 2 — Synopsis; Objectives, Endpoints, and Estimands
changes.summary: Minor update to the name of the secondary endpoints 'time [days] to sustained resolution of all targeted COVID-19 signs/symptoms' and 'Duration of each targeted COVID-19 sign/symptom' to remove 'sign'.
changes.rationale: For consistency in how targeted symptom data from the e-Diary is described (to be consistent with 'Targeted Symptoms for Analysis' in Appendix 10 Table 5).

changes.name: 2 — Schedule of Activities; Nasal / NP Swab for Viral Screening Assessment
changes.summary: Text added to clarify the procedure if a participant has a negative RAT at screening and there is a strong suspicion that the participant has COVID-19.
changes.rationale: To reduce the chances of screen failure in participants that have COVID-19 when the screening RAT fails to correctly identify the infection (PACL dated 03 Feb 2025).

changedSections.sectionTitle: Schedule of Activities; Nasal / NP Swab for Viral Screening Assessment

changes.name: 2 — China
changes.summary: China Country specific appendix added, which includes information on human genetic resource samples collected in China and clarification on the management of retained research samples in China.
changes.rationale: To comply with HGRAC requirements (PACL dated 14 May 2025).

changes.name: 2 — Signs and Symptoms Attributable to COVID-19
changes.summary: Included Fatigue as a Targeted Symptom for Analysis.
changes.rationale: To allow for fatigue to be utilized in analyses of targeted COVID-19 symptoms.

changedSections.sectionTitle: Signs and Symptoms Attributable to COVID-19

versions.rationale: Evaluate efficacy and safety of ibuzatrelvir in non-hospitalized symptomatic adult and adolescent participants with COVID-19 who are at high risk of progression to severe illness.

population.name: Main Population
population.label: This study will recruit symptomatic, non-hospitalized COVID-19 infected adults and adolescents who are at risk of progression to severe disease.

notes.text: includesHealthySubjects inferred as false because the study recruits symptomatic, non-hospitalized COVID-19 infected participants (disease-specific population).

cohorts.name: Individuals aged 12 to 49 years
cohorts.label: Participation by individuals aged 12 to 49 years will be limited to no more than approximately 20% of the total population.
cohorts.description: To ensure a reasonably balanced distribution across age groups and risk factors, participation by individuals aged 12 to 49 years will be limited to no more than approximately 20% of the total population.

cohorts.name: Individuals aged 50 to 64 years
cohorts.label: Participation by individuals aged 50 to 64 years will be limited to no more than approximately 20% of the total population.
cohorts.description: To ensure a reasonably balanced distribution across age groups and risk factors, participation by individuals aged 50 to 64 years will be limited to no more than approximately 20% of the total population. Participants aged 50 to 64 years who present with any of the following will not be included in this enrollment cap: moderate immunocompromise, cardiovascular disease, chronic lung disease, or mild to moderate renal impairment.

population.description:
This study will recruit symptomatic, non-hospitalized COVID-19 infected adults and adolescents who are at risk of progression to severe disease. Risk factors may include advanced age, obesity, cigarette smoking, diabetes, moderate immunosuppression, chronic lung disease and cardiovascular disease, among others. Participants will also need to be unable to receive nirmatrelvir/ritonavir as SoC for reasons such as intolerance, drug interactions, unavailability or refusal based on concerns around its safety and tolerability profile. Patients with uncontrolled medical conditions, severe immunosuppression, or severe COVID-19 that requires hospitalization at presentation will not be able to participate in this trial.

To ensure a reasonably balanced distribution across age groups and risk factors, participation by individuals aged 12 to 49 years and 50 to 64 years will each be limited to no more than approximately 20% of the total population. Participants aged 50 to 64 years who present with any of the following will not be included in this enrollment cap: moderate immunocompromise, cardiovascular disease, chronic lung disease, or mild to moderate renal impairment.

arms.name: Treatment arm
arms.label: Treatment arm
arms.description: Participants will receive 600 mg twice a day (BID) on Day 1 to Day 5 (10 doses in total)

arms.name: Placebo arm
arms.label: Placebo arm
arms.description: Participants will receive 0 mg BID on Day 1 to Day 5 (10 doses in total)

studyDesigns.name: Randomized Double-Blind Parallel-Group Design
studyDesigns.label: Phase 3, superiority, adaptive, 2-arm, parallel group, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter study in non-hospitalized, symptomatic, adult and adolescent participants with COVID-19 who are at high risk of progressing to severe illness, to determine the efficacy, safety, and tolerability of ibuzatrelvir.

epochs.name: Screening Period
epochs.label: Screening Period
epochs.description: Screening period up to 1 day before randomization. Screening procedures may be completed on the same calendar day as randomization and Baseline/Day 1 procedures, including first dose of study intervention. Informed consent / assent must be obtained prior to undergoing any study-specific procedures.

epochs.name: Treatment Period
epochs.label: Treatment Period
epochs.description: Study intervention administration through Day 5; participants will be randomized (1:1) to receive ibuzatrelvir or placebo orally twice daily for 5 days (10 doses total). Baseline assessments must be performed before a participant takes their first dose of study intervention.

epochs.name: Follow-up Period
epochs.label: Follow-up Period
epochs.description: After the 5-day treatment phase, participants will be followed up until Week 24 for safety and efficacy endpoint assessments. The F/U will continue monthly from Week 8 through Week 24 with telemedicine visits at Week 8, Week 16, and Week 20 and in person visits at Week 12 and Week 24, for the assessment of secondary efficacy endpoints.

elements.name: EL1
elements.label: Informed consent, and assent (if appropriate)
elements.description: Informed consent / assent must be obtained prior to undergoing any study-specific procedures. See Section 10.1.3.

transitionStartRule.name: Informed consent start
transitionStartRule.text: Informed consent / assent must be obtained prior to undergoing any study-specific procedures.

elements.name: EL4
elements.label: Complete/Targeted physical examination
elements.description: Complete examination to be performed at screening. All subsequent examinations may be targeted, or symptoms driven.

elements.name: EL5
elements.label: Study intervention administration
elements.description: Baseline assessments must be performed before a participant takes their first dose of study intervention. If only one dose was administered on Day 1, study intervention administration should end on Day 6.

transitionEndRule.name: Study intervention end
transitionEndRule.text: If only one dose was administered on Day 1, study intervention administration should end on Day 6.

elements.name: EL6
elements.label: PK sample (ibuzatrelvir)
elements.description: On Day 1, one blood sample for PK will be collected 30 to 90 minutes post-dose. On Day 5, one blood sample for PK will be collected. The preferred time of sample collection is pre-dose up to 2 hours before study intervention administration; if a pre-dose sample collection is not possible, collect this sample at any time during the visit, even after study intervention has been administered.

transitionEndRule.name: PK sample Day 5 end
transitionEndRule.text: On Day 5, one blood sample for PK will be collected.

transitionStartRule.name: PK sample Day 1 start
transitionStartRule.text: On Day 1, one blood sample for PK will be collected 30 to 90 minutes post-dose.

elements.name: EL7
elements.label: Participant-completed study diary (COVID-19 symptoms and global impression questions)
elements.description: COVID-19 symptoms will be assessed once daily every day from Day 1 through Day 28. Global impression questions will be answered every day from Day 1 through Day 28 after COVID-19 symptom diary is completed. See Section 8.2.5.1, and Section 8.2.5.2.

elements.name: EL8
elements.label: Participant-completed study diary (Long COVID symptoms), global impression questions and PROMIS instrument anchors
elements.description: Long COVID symptoms, global impression questions and PROMIS instruments will be answered at Baseline and weekly from Day 29 (Week 4) through Week 24. Only adult participants (≥18 years of age at screening) will be asked to complete the global impression questions for Long COVID/PASC. See Section 8.2.5.5, Section 8.2.5.6, and Table 2 for F/U from Week 8 through Week 24.

elements.name: EL9
elements.label: SARS-CoV-2 RNA level assessment (NP)
elements.description: Day 1: NP sample must be collected prior to first dose of study intervention. Day 3: A nasal swab will be self-collected by the participant at home and either returned to the site at Day 5 or shipped to central lab via the courier. In addition to the required sample collections indicated, from Day 5 to Day 34 an NP swab for viral RNA sample should be collected within 48 hours of COVID-19 symptom onset if a participant experiences moderate or severe symptom rebound. Collect at an unscheduled visit if there is no scheduled visit within 48 hours. In addition to the time points indicated, NP sample may be collected at Day 21 and Day 34 if the participant presents with new onset of COVID-19 symptoms after prior resolution, or if symptoms persist from baseline visit.

transitionStartRule.name: NP RNA assessment start
transitionStartRule.text: Day 1: NP sample must be collected prior to first dose of study intervention.

elements.name: EL10
elements.label: Enrollment (randomization/ study intervention assignment)
elements.description: At randomization/study intervention assignment, the participant enrollment number and allocation are assigned.

elements.name: EL11
elements.label: Vital status check
elements.description: Verify vital status of participants. See Section 8.2.2.

estimands.name: Primary Estimand
estimands.description: The difference in proportions of patients requiring COVID-19 related emergency department visits with administration of supplemental oxygen, COVID-19 antiviral or IV treatment (eg, hydration, antibiotics, or corticosteroids), all-cause hospitalization, or all- cause death through Day 28 between ibuzatrelvir and placebo, among patients who were treated ≤5 days after COVID-19 symptom onset and who were not receiving background SoC treatment for their COVID-19 infection at baseline (ie, initiated at randomization). Participants who discontinue from study before Day 28 or are lost to follow-up, will be imputed using multiple imputation. The use of rescue medication will be taken into consideration as an intercurrent event. This will be estimated without regard to adherence to randomized treatment. Only adjudicated data will be used for the analysis of COVID-19-related emergency department visits and rescue medication data.
estimands.populationSummary: Non‑hospitalized symptomatic adult and adolescent patients at high risk of progression treated ≤5 days after symptom onset and not receiving background SoC at baseline (mITT Analysis Set).

intercurrentEvents.name: Discontinuation or lost to follow-up
intercurrentEvents.text: For participants who discontinue from study before Day 28 or are lost to follow-up, missing data will be imputed using multiple imputation.

intercurrentEvents.name: Rescue medication use
intercurrentEvents.text: If a participant starts rescue medication and does not have a composite primary endpoint event through Day 28: if rescue medication is administered because of worsening COVID-19, the participant will be considered as having a composite primary endpoint event with the rescue medication start date as the event date; if rescue medication is administered not because of worsening COVID-19, the participant's data will be considered to be missing from the start of rescue medication onwards and will be imputed using multiple imputation.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Composite event if rescue due to worsening; otherwise missing imputed by MI

intercurrentEvents.name: Non-adherence to randomized treatment
intercurrentEvents.text: Treatment effect will be estimated without regard to adherence to randomized treatment.

estimands.name: Key Secondary Estimand 1
estimands.description: The difference in median time (days) to sustained resolution of all targeted COVID-19 symptoms through Day 28 between ibuzatrelvir and placebo, for patients who were treated ≤5 days after COVID-19 symptom onset and who were not receiving background SoC treatment for their COVID-19 infection at baseline (ie, initiated at randomization). For a participant who discontinues from the study before Day 28 without symptom resolution, the censoring date will be at the last date on which symptom resolution is assessed or Day 27 whichever occurs first. Participants with a composite primary endpoint event, or who started rescue medication because of worsening COVID-19 through Day 28 will be classified as not achieving symptom resolution and will be censored at Day 27. This will be estimated without regard to adherence to randomized treatment.
estimands.populationSummary: Non‑hospitalized symptomatic adult and adolescent patients at high risk treated ≤5 days after symptom onset and not receiving background SoC at baseline (mITT Analysis Set).

intercurrentEvents.name: Discontinuation or lost to follow-up
intercurrentEvents.text: For participants who discontinue before Day 28 or are lost to follow-up, missing data will be imputed using multiple imputation.

intercurrentEvents.name: Rescue medication use
intercurrentEvents.text: Participants with a composite primary endpoint event, or who started rescue medication because of worsening COVID-19 through Day 28 will be classified as not achieving symptom resolution and will be censored at Day 27.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Censoring at Day 27 if rescue due to worsening

estimands.name: Key Secondary Estimand 2 - CRPE
estimands.description: The difference in the proportions of patients with CRPE from Day 1 to Week 24 between ibuzatrelvir and placebo, for patients who were treated ≤5 days after COVID-19 symptom onset and who were not receiving background SoC treatment for their COVID-19 infection at baseline (ie, initiated at randomization). This will be estimated without regard to adherence to randomized treatment. For participants who discontinue from study before Week 24 or are lost to follow-up, missing data will be imputed using multiple imputation or other appropriate imputation methods.
estimands.populationSummary: Non‑hospitalized symptomatic adult and adolescent patients at high risk treated ≤5 days after symptom onset and not receiving background SoC at baseline (mITT Analysis Set).

intercurrentEvents.name: Discontinuation or lost to follow-up
intercurrentEvents.text: For participants who discontinue from study before Week 24 or are lost to follow-up, missing data will be imputed using multiple imputation or other appropriate imputation methods.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Multiple imputation or other appropriate methods

intercurrentEvents.name: Non-adherence to randomized treatment
intercurrentEvents.text: Treatment effect will be estimated without regard to adherence to randomized treatment.

estimands.name: Key Secondary Estimand 3 - PACE
estimands.description: The difference in proportions of patients with PACE from Day 29 to Week 24 between ibuzatrelvir and placebo, for patients who were treated ≤5 days after COVID-19 symptom onset and who were not receiving background SoC treatment for their COVID-19 infection at baseline (ie, initiated at randomization). This will be estimated without regard to adherence to randomized treatment. For participants who discontinue from study before Week 24 or are lost to follow-up, missing data will be imputed using multiple imputation or other appropriate imputation methods.
estimands.populationSummary: Non‑hospitalized symptomatic adult and adolescent patients at high risk treated ≤5 days after symptom onset and not receiving background SoC at baseline (mITT Analysis Set).

intercurrentEvents.name: Discontinuation or lost to follow-up
intercurrentEvents.text: For participants who discontinue from study before Week 24 or are lost to follow-up, missing data will be imputed using multiple imputation or other appropriate imputation methods.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Multiple imputation or other appropriate methods

estimands.name: Key Secondary Estimand 4 - Long COVID/PASC
estimands.description: The difference in proportions of patients with Long COVID/PASC symptoms at Week 24, based on Long COVID/PASC PRO between ibuzatrelvir and placebo treatment groups. For participants who discontinued from the study, if their last Long COVID/PASC PRO was completed at Week 12 or later, then the last completed assessment will be utilized in the analysis. However, if the last completed assessment was prior to Week 12, the participant's Long COVID/PASC PRO assessment will not be utilized and will be considered missing and will not be imputed. This will be estimated without regard to adherence to randomized treatment.
estimands.populationSummary: Non‑hospitalized symptomatic adult and adolescent patients at high risk treated ≤5 days after symptom onset and not receiving background SoC at baseline (mITT Analysis Set).

intercurrentEvents.name: Study discontinuation affecting Long COVID PRO
intercurrentEvents.text: For the intercurrent event of discontinuation from the study, only Long COVID/PASC PROs completed at Week 12 or later will be utilized in the analysis; earlier assessments will be considered missing and not imputed.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Use last PRO if at Week 12 or later; otherwise consider missing (no imputation)

studyDesigns.rationale: Ibuzatrelvir is a potent and selective inhibitor of the severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) main protease (M pro ) with low risk for drug-drug interactions (DDIs). The purpose of the study is to evaluate the efficacy and safety of ibuzatrelvir for the treatment of non-hospitalized, symptomatic, adult and adolescent participants with COVID-19 who are at high risk of progressing to severe illness.

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_01
objectives.text: Compare the efficacy of ibuzatrelvir versus placebo to prevent COVID-19‑related emergency department visits requiring oxygen/antiviral/IV treatment, all‑cause hospitalization, or death through Day 28 in non‑hospitalized symptomatic participants at high risk of progression to severe illness.

endpoints.name: EP_PR_01
endpoints.text: Proportion of participants with COVID-19‑related emergency department visits with administration of supplemental oxygen, COVID‑19 antiviral or IV treatment, all‑cause hospitalization, or all‑cause death through Day 28.
endpoints.description: The composite primary endpoint is the proportion of participants with COVID-19-related emergency department visits with administration of supplemental oxygen, COVID-19 antiviral or IV treatment (eg, hydration, antibiotics, or corticosteroids), all-cause hospitalization, or all-cause death through Day 28.

objectives.description: To compare the efficacy of ibuzatrelvir to placebo in non-hospitalized participants with COVID-19 who are at high risk of progression to severe illness.

objectives.name: OBJ_EX_01
objectives.text: Determine ibuzatrelvir pharmacokinetics in non‑hospitalized participants with COVID‑19 at high risk of progression.
objectives.description: To determine the PK of ibuzatrelvir in non- hospitalized participants with COVID-19 who are at high risk of progression to severe illness.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_01
objectives.text: Compare the effect of ibuzatrelvir versus placebo on the severity and time to sustained resolution of targeted COVID‑19 symptoms through Day 28 in non‑hospitalized symptomatic participants at high risk.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_01
endpoints.text: Time (days) to sustained resolution of all targeted COVID‑19 symptoms through Day 28 (assessed over two consecutive days).
endpoints.description: Time (days) to sustained resolution of all targeted COVID-19 symptoms through Day 28.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_01B
endpoints.text: Time (days) to sustained alleviation of targeted COVID‑19 symptoms through Day 28 (as defined by change from baseline severity to mild/absent over two consecutive days).
endpoints.description: Time (days) to sustained alleviation of all targeted COVID-19 symptoms, as assessed over 2 consecutive days, through Day 28. Alleviation of all targeted COVID-19 symptoms is defined as the event occurring on the first of two consecutive days when all symptoms scored as moderate or severe at study entry are scored as mild or absent AND all symptoms scored mild or absent at study entry are scored as absent.

objectives.description: To compare the severity of COVID-19 symptoms of ibuzatrelvir to placebo in non- hospitalized participants with COVID-19 who are at high risk of progression to severe illness.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_02
objectives.text: Compare ibuzatrelvir versus placebo for prevention of prespecified cardiovascular, renal, and pulmonary events (CRPE), post‑acute COVID‑19 events (PACE), and Long COVID/PASC up to Week 24 in non‑hospitalized symptomatic participants at high risk.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_02A
endpoints.text: Proportion of participants with CRPE from Day 1 to Week 24.
endpoints.description: Proportion of participants with CRPE from Day 1 to Week 24.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_02B
endpoints.text: Proportion of participants with PACE from Day 29 to Week 24.
endpoints.description: Proportion of participants with PACE from Day 29 to Week 24.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_02C
endpoints.text: Proportion of participants with Long COVID/PASC symptoms at Week 24 based on the Long COVID/PASC patient‑reported outcomes (PRO).
endpoints.description: Proportion of participants with Long COVID/PASC symptoms at Week 24, based on the Long COVID/PASC PRO.

objectives.description: To compare the efficacy of ibuzatrelvir to placebo in preventing the onset of prespecified cardiovascular, renal, and pulmonary events (CRPE), post-acute COVID-19 events (PACE), and post-acute sequelae of COVID- 19 (PASC), or Long COVID, in non- hospitalized participants with COVID-19 who are at high risk of progression to severe illness.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_03
objectives.text: Describe the effect of ibuzatrelvir versus placebo on SARS‑CoV‑2 RNA levels and virologic rebound in NP/nasal samples over time.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_03A
endpoints.text: Change from baseline in SARS‑CoV‑2 RNA in NP/nasal samples over time.
endpoints.description: Change from baseline in SARS- CoV-2 RNA in NP/nasal samples over time.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_03B
endpoints.text: Proportion of participants with rebound in SARS‑CoV‑2 RNA levels in NP samples at Day 10 or Day 14; and proportion with symptomatic and virologic rebound through Day 28.
endpoints.description: Proportion of participants with rebound in SARS-CoV-2 RNA levels in NP samples at Day 10 or Day 14 (Rebound as defined in Appendix 12). Proportion of participants with symptomatic and virologic rebound through Day 28.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_03C
endpoints.text: Time to viral RNA <LLOQ and proportion with viral RNA <LLOQ at each visit.
endpoints.description: Time to viral RNA <LLOQ. Proportion of participants with viral RNA <LLOQ at each visit.

objectives.description: To describe the effect of ibuzatrelvir treatment versus placebo on SARS-CoV-2 ribonucleic acid (RNA) levels in nasopharyngeal (NP)/nasal samples in non-hospitalized participants with COVID-19 who are at high risk of progression to severe illness.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_04
objectives.text: Describe the safety and tolerability of ibuzatrelvir versus placebo in non‑hospitalized symptomatic participants with COVID‑19 who are at high risk of progression.

endpoints.name: EP_SAF_01
endpoints.text: Incidence of treatment‑emergent adverse events (TEAEs), serious adverse events (SAEs), and AEs leading to discontinuation.
endpoints.description: Incidence of TEAEs. Incidence of SAEs and AEs leading to discontinuations.

objectives.description: To describe the safety and tolerability of ibuzatrelvir relative to placebo in non- hospitalized participants with COVID-19 who are at high risk of progression to severe illness.

scheduleTimelines.name: Main Timeline
scheduleTimelines.label: Main Timeline
scheduleTimelines.description: Main timeline for the study design.
scheduleTimelines.entryCondition: Informed consent / assent must be obtained prior to undergoing any study-specific procedures.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Current need or anticipated need for hospitalization within 24 hours, due to sign
eligibilityCriteria.text: Current need or anticipated need for hospitalization within 24 hours, due to signs of severe COVID-19 illness (eg, SpO2 <94% on room air, respiratory rate >30 breaths/minute, or lung infiltrates >50%) or due to other medical conditions requiring hospitalization in the opinion of the site investigator.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection as determined by RAT in nasal or NP specimen collecte
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection as determined by RAT in nasal or NP specimen collected within 1 day prior to randomization. Initial onset of symptoms attributable to COVID-19 within 5 days prior to randomization and at least 1 of the specified symptoms attributable to COVID-19 present on the day of randomization (see Appendix 10 for criteria). Randomization must occur no later than the 5th day, where the onset of symptoms is the first day.

Participants must be unable or unwilling to take nirmatrelvir/ritonavir treatment for reasons listed in Appendix 11.

eligibilityCriteria.name: 12 to <18 years of age, weighing at least 40 kg (where permitted by local regula
eligibilityCriteria.text: 12 to <18 years of age, weighing at least 40 kg (where permitted by local regulation), or ≥18 years of age of any weight at screening.

eligibilityCriteria.name: WOCBP may be enrolled if they agree to use a highly effective method of contracept
eligibilityCriteria.text: WOCBP may be enrolled if they agree to use a highly effective method of contraception. Refer to Appendix 4 for reproductive criteria for male (Section 10.4.1) and female (Section 10.4.2) participants.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Presence of risk factors for progression to severe COVID-19 at the time of scree
Receiving dialysis or have known severe renal impairment (ie, eGFR consistently <30
eligibilityCriteria.text: Receiving dialysis or have known severe renal impairment (ie, eGFR consistently <30 mL/min/1.73 m2 for adults or CrCl <30 mL/min for adolescents, using the serum creatinine-based CKD-EPI formula or the Cockroft Gault, respectively).

eligibilityCriteria.name: Active liver disease with AST or ALT >3 ULN, Total bilirubin ≥2 × ULN (for G
eligibilityCriteria.text: Active liver disease with AST or ALT >3 ULN, Total bilirubin ≥2 × ULN (for Gilbert's syndrome, direct bilirubin >ULN is exclusionary) within the past 3 months, or liver function impairment with Class C per Child Pugh classification.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Suspected or confirmed concurrent active systemic infection other than COVID-19 th
eligibilityCriteria.text: Suspected or confirmed concurrent active systemic infection other than COVID-19 that may interfere with the evaluation of response to the study intervention.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Ongoing Long COVID or PASC diagnosis.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Ongoing Long COVID or PASC diagnosis.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Any comorbidity requiring hospitalization and/or surgery within 7 days prior to stu
eligibilityCriteria.text: Any comorbidity requiring hospitalization and/or surgery within 7 days prior to study entry, or that is considered life threatening within 30 days prior to study entry, as determined by the investigator.

eligibilityCriteria.name: History of hypersensitivity or other contraindication to any of the components
eligibilityCriteria.text: History of hypersensitivity or other contraindication to any of the components of the study interventions.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Any medical or psychiatric condition including recent (within the past year) or act
eligibilityCriteria.text: Any medical or psychiatric condition including recent (within the past year) or active suicidal ideation/behavior or laboratory abnormality that may increase the risk of study participation or, in the investigator's judgment, make the participant inappropriate for the study.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Current use of any prohibited concomitant medication(s). Refer to Section 6.9.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Current use of any prohibited concomitant medication(s). Refer to Section 6.9.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Has received any other antiviral for the treatment of COVID-19, including remde
eligibilityCriteria.text: Has received any other antiviral for the treatment of COVID-19, including remdesivir, nirmatrelvir/ritonavir, molnupiravir, or COVID-19 mAbs within 30 days or 5 half-lives [whichever is longer] prior to screening, or received convalescent COVID-19 plasma within 12 months.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Received any dose of a COVID-19 vaccine within 4 months of randomization or ex
eligibilityCriteria.text: Received any dose of a COVID-19 vaccine within 4 months of randomization or expected to receive one through Day 34.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Previous administration of an investigational product (drug or vaccine) withi
eligibilityCriteria.text: Previous administration of an investigational product (drug or vaccine) within 30 days or 5 half-lives preceding the first dose of study intervention used in this study (whichever is longer).

eligibilityCriteria.name: Prior participation in this clinical trial or any other clinical trial of ibuza
eligibilityCriteria.text: Prior participation in this clinical trial or any other clinical trial of ibuzatrelvir.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Investigator site staff directly involved in the conduct of the study and their
eligibilityCriteria.text: Investigator site staff directly involved in the conduct of the study and their family members, site staff otherwise supervised by the investigator, and sponsor and sponsor delegate employees directly involved in the conduct of the study and their family members.

studyInterventions.name: Ibuzatrelvir — Treatment arm
studyInterventions.description: Investigational product administered to evaluate efficacy and safety in symptomatic non-hospitalized COVID-19 patients at risk of progression.

administrations.name: Ibuzatrelvir 600 mg orally every 12 hours for 5 days (10 doses)
administrations.description: Two 300 mg tablets taken together (within 5 minutes). First dose administered at site; subsequent doses may be self-administered at home. Take every 12 hours (±4 hours), not more than twice in a calendar day. May be taken with or without food. Swallow whole; do not manipulate or chew. If a dose is delayed, take as soon as possible but no later than 4 hours before next scheduled dose. Stop dosing at end of treatment period (10 doses total).

studyInterventions.name: Placebo — Placebo arm
studyInterventions.description: Matching placebo administered on the same schedule as active treatment for blinding and comparator purposes.

administrations.name: Matching placebo orally every 12 hours for 5 days (10 doses)
administrations.description: Two matching placebo tablets taken together (within 5 minutes). First dose administered at site; subsequent doses may be self-administered at home. Take every 12 hours (±4 hours), not more than twice in a calendar day. Swallow whole; do not manipulate or chew. Stop dosing at end of treatment period (10 doses total).
